💲💲 Capital Management Tips for Beginners 💲💲

When placing a trade, risk only 2%–5% of your account per order, depending on your risk appetite.

📩 Example: Let’s say your account is $1,000 → 2% risk = $20 → with 20x leverage, that gives you a position size of $400.

If your SL is 1%, that's a $4 loss. Your TP at 3% = $12 gain.

Even if you hit SL three times in a row (3 trades = 3% account loss), just one winning trade with a 3% return can cover all the losses.

That’s why every long and short setup we share comes with an R:R (Risk:Reward) ratio of 1:5 to 1:10

➡️ MUST READ: Smaller SLs with bigger TPs help reduce emotional pressure and increase both your win rate and capital growth, as long as you stick to proper risk management.
Sweep sl -3.71%, wait to cover soon
ARB sweep sl - 24.6 %, wait to cover

In this case, you can see that $STRK pumped with 25.4% PnL with 10x leverage

However, it eventually hit SL. So as a trader, what actions should you do in this case?

Simple, this is our private trading habit when controlling a profit signal ⤵️

1️⃣ Set up SL, TP
2️⃣ When the signal has profit, you set your SL to your entry price. This action is to secure your capital when the signal dumps, you will only lost your profit, not your capital
3️⃣ Always take profit 20-30% when you have positive PnL. 
- If it continues to increase, take another 30% TP, and keep it going until you find it done
- If it dumps back, you already TP 30% and close the signal at the entry level

So what happened if it hits SL right before the profit has profit?
=> This will belong to the capital management skill, which we have been coaching our private members to follow and make sure you will lose the with the least money.

Trading is that simple right? 🎆
